author: @Jarni1979
text: It's called cartapesta, not Carta Pasta.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Thanks for your kind comments - I love this technique. I found out about it when i was living in Leche in southern Italy and they seem to keep it a huge secret - I'm not surprised. They use coloured papers in the same way - that way you don't even need to paint them and the finished results are something else. If you ever get a chance to go there you should - you will learn an awful lot about how to do this and extend it from just a couple of days there. Anyway, I wish I had time to more

author: @MllMargsy
text: The result is quite beautiful! I've always done papier mâché strip by strip but I love the idea of using a large piece for draping... I'll definatively have to try this :D
